title: First report of Fusarium equiseti causing crown and root rot of cucumber in India
abstract:
In 2018–2020, during surveys of approximately 160 ha in
15 cucumber growing locations across the Solan District of
Himachal Pradesh, the crop was found to be infected by a root
and crown rot fungus causing symptoms such as the yellowing
and wilting of leaves in 5–15% of plants. Two–three days
after the isolations were made from root, crown and stem
portions (Aldakil et al. 2019), in 60% of 40 samples colonies
grew out that were initially white, aerial and floccose and
later on turned yellowish to buff brown in colour....
